markdown:
What if we press the button ten times?
What number will come up? How will it have? What should we call it?

Many zeroes: The number will be 100 lakhs, which is also called a crore. 1 crore is written as 1,00,00,000 — it is 1 followed by seven zeroes.

# 1.3
Of Crores and Crores!

The table below shows numbers according to both the Indian and the American (also called the International) system of naming numerals and placing commas.
Notice that in the Indian system, commas are placed to group digits in a 3-2-2-2... pattern from right to left (thousands, lakhs, crores, etc.). In the American system, the digits are grouped in a 3-3-3-3... pattern from right to left (thousands, millions, billions, etc.).
The Indian system of naming numbers is also followed in Bhutan, Nepal, Sri Lanka, Pakistan, Bangladesh, Maldives, Afghanistan, and Myanmar.
The words lakh and crore originate from the Sanskrit words laksha (लक्ष) and koti (कोटि). The American system is also used in many countries.
Observe the number of zeroes in 1 lakh and 1 crore.
1 lakh, written in numbers would be 1 followed by 5 zeroes.
1 crore, written in numbers would be 1 followed by 7 zeroes.
A lakh is a hundred times a thousand, a crore is a hundred times a lakh, and an arab is a hundred times a crore (i.e., a hundred thousand is 1 lakh, 100 lakhs is 1 crore, and 100 crores is 1 arab).
How many zeros does a thousand lakh have?
